utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with costs: (1) health_coverage: includes physical and mental health insurance with explicit monthly costs and a total health cost—fully specified. (2) property_coverage: specifies home/contents insurance with a monthly cost—fully specified. (3) travel_coverage: includes travel insurance with costs (base and add-on) and total travel cost—fully specified. (4) additional_services: lists enhancement services and notes inclusions; some do not have explicit costs, but the required format says “any enhancement services with costs.” Since costs are not provided for these services (they are listed as included/discount-based), this may be considered incomplete. However, as they are enhancement services and some are included at no additional cost and a discount is specified (10%), this can be interpreted as costs provided (zero or discount). (5) total_budget: provides complete monthly/annual cost breakdown and itemized costs—fully specified. Overall, 5 items included; additional_services arguably acceptable given stated included-without-cost and discount.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists specific products: LifeFirst Standard Plan for physical health, LifeFirst Standard Plan for mental health, LifeFirst Standard Coverage for home contents, InsurePlus Standard Plan for travel insurance, and an Adventure Travel Coverage add-on. I matched each item name exactly to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings under the appropriate categories: primary_health_coverage (physical_health and mental_health), property_protection (home_content), mobility_coverage (travel_insurance), and travel_enhancement_services. Then I extracted the exact rating numbers for those items.
